name: The Seven Factors of Awakening
intro: Seven factors that ripen into awakening — four you already hold (sati, pīti, samādhi, upekkhā), three that complete the set.

words.gloss: investigation of phenomena; discerning what is happening

etymology.note: investigation as a lamp, not a doubt — looking closely at experience while it happens
not strain but heroic persistence — the energy that keeps returning to the object

words.gloss: tranquility; calm of body and mind

etymology.note: the calm that makes happiness possible — quieter than pīti, deeper than comfort

canonicalLine.pali: passaddhakāyo sukhaṃ vedeti, sukhino cittaṃ samādhiyati
canonicalLine.translation: tranquil in body, one feels happiness; the mind of one who is happy becomes concentrated

talk.text: Awakening has seven factors, and they balance like a fire.
looks closely — what is actually happening right now?
brings wood when the mind is dull, and
rises with the flame. When the fire runs hot,
gathers the light to one point, and
lets it burn evenly, needing nothing.

suttaRec.title: Gilāna — Sick
suttaRec.blurb: Mahākassapa is gravely ill; the Buddha recites the seven factors of awakening, and he rises cured. The origin of the bojjhaṅga healing chants.
